steps.title: Follow the less-need comparison
steps.content: The speaker says she has less need now than when she was young. The underlined lines then identify what she feels less need to do: share herself with everyone and turn her thoughts into spoken words.

steps.title: Reject misread functions
steps.content:
• Choice A turns the lines into advice about acquaintances, but the speaker is describing herself.
  • Choice C treats the lines as a remembered event, not examples of changed inclinations.
  • Choice D makes the behaviors current social habits, while the text says she has less need for them now.
The answer is B.
